methodology: GW38 is the final gameweek. All 10 fixtures kick off simultaneously. FDR ratings 1-5 (1=easiest). Away venue adds +0.3 weighted penalty. No multi-GW window applicable — single-fixture GW. Squad players cross-referenced against fixture table. xpts data from cache is uniformly 0 (partial model run); FDR used as primary signal.

fixtures.notes: Arsenal host Everton (relegated). Gunners have strong clean sheet potential. Everton have nothing to play for, likely to rotate.
Bournemouth at home to mid-table Palace. Good fixture for Bournemouth assets. Palace away is moderate.
Brentford host Man City. Hard home fixture for Brentford. Man City away at Brentford is manageable but City rotation risk is elevated if title race is settled.
Brighton host a struggling Man Utd. Easy home game for Brighton assets. Man Utd away is tough given poor season form.
Balanced clash between two solid mid/upper-table sides. Neither team has a clear fixture advantage.
Fulham host relegated Wolves. Excellent attacking fixture for Fulham. Wolves away have nothing to play for.
Liverpool away at Ipswich. Standout fixture for Liverpool assets.
Competitive top-half fixture. Both sides likely chasing European places. Balanced difficulty.
Spurs host West Ham. Good home fixture for Tottenham attackers. West Ham away is a moderate ask.
Burnley vs Sunderland — both teams with limited ceiling. Bench GK Dubravka unlikely to register meaningful returns.

fixture_ratings.verdict: BEST — FDR 1 away at Ipswich. Prime captaincy fixture.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2 vs relegated Everton. Strong CS and attacking potential.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2 vs relegated Wolves.
EXCELLENT — home FDR 2. Man Utd have had a poor season.
GOOD — home FDR 2 vs West Ham.
GOOD fixture but HEAVY rotation risk. Haaland start not guaranteed.
NEUTRAL — FDR 3 at home vs Villa.
NEUTRAL/AVOID — FDR 3 away at Stamford Bridge.
AVOID — FDR 3 away at Spurs. Bowen faces a tough away trip.
BAD — hosting Man City. Thiago faces a hard fixture.
BAD — FDR 4 away at Brighton. B.Fernandes and Casemiro face a tough fixture.
WORST — hosting Liverpool. Avoid Ipswich assets entirely.

Joint-best fixture. Arsenal attack vs relegated Everton at home.
Joint-best fixture. Midfield CS bonus potential plus goal involvement.
Good home fixture. Brighton vs a struggling Man Utd offers CS potential.
Decent home fixture. Bournemouth CS record is inconsistent.
Good fixture on paper (FDR 2 away) but ROTATION RISK is the primary concern. City may rest Haaland on final day.
Neutral fixture. Newcastle home vs Villa is competitive — CS not guaranteed.
Tough away trip. Forest at Chelsea is a difficult fixture for attacking returns.
Hard away fixture. Bowen as captain is questionable — WHU away at Spurs. Current captain per squad.json.
Second-worst squad fixture. Brentford host Man City — difficult for attacking returns.
Worst squad fixture. Man Utd away at Brighton (FDR 4) with low motivation.

bench_fixture_summary.note: Good fixture but City rotation risk. May not feature.
Moderate away fixture. Palace consistency unreliable.
Poor fixture. Likely rested on final day.

bench_fixture_summary.note: Bench GK. Low ceiling regardless of fixture. No actionable value.

best_runs.note: Arsenal have the best squad fixture — home vs relegated Everton. Three starting assets covering DEF clean sheet, attacking MID, and set-piece threat.
Brighton home vs Man Utd is an excellent GK/DEF fixture.

worst_runs.note: Man Utd away at Brighton is FDR 4 with motivation risk. Worst fixtures in the squad.
Brentford home vs Man City is the second-worst squad fixture. Low attacking ceiling.

rotation_warnings: Man City (Haaland, O'Reilly): heavy rotation expected if title position settled. Haaland rest is plausible — Gemini also flagged this as the key risk.
Man Utd (B.Fernandes, Casemiro): low-motivation final day, tough away fixture at Brighton.
West Ham (Bowen): away at Spurs with season potentially settled. Motivation variable. Currently named captain — switching to Saka is strongly recommended on fixture grounds.
Any European-qualified team may rest key players if CL/EL spots fully locked in before GW38.
captaincy_note: Bowen is the current captain but faces WHU away at Spurs (weighted 3.3) — the 7th-best squad fixture. Saka and Rice both have weighted FDR 2.0 (home vs relegated Everton) and represent a clear fixture upgrade. Gemini recommends switching to Haaland (weighted 2.3, but rotation risk). If Haaland is confirmed to start, his ceiling is higher; if not, Arsenal trio is the safer floor.
best_def_run: Arsenal home vs Everton — best CS probability for any squad DEF/GK. Gabriel (VC) is the top defensive asset for GW38.
notes: GW38 is a simultaneous single-day event — all 10 fixtures at the same kick-off. No deadline advantage from staggered KOs.
No DGW or BGW teams in GW38. Every team plays exactly once.
FDR scale: 1=very easy, 2=easy, 3=medium, 4=hard, 5=very hard. Away penalty of +0.3 applied to weighted score.
xpts data in cache is uniformly 0 for squad players — FDR is the primary signal for this report.
Rotation risk is the biggest wild card for GW38. Confirm team news at deadline.
All chips have been used. No chip levers available.
summary: GW38 has no DGW or BGW complications — all teams play once. Best squad fixture: Arsenal trio (Gabriel/Saka/Rice) home vs relegated Everton (weighted FDR 2.0). Worst squad fixtures: B.Fernandes away at Brighton (FDR 4.3) and Thiago home vs Man City (FDR 4.0). Captain risk: Bowen faces WHU away at Spurs (3.3) — switching to Saka is warranted on fixture grounds. Rotation risk at Man City is the primary GW38 uncertainty for Haaland.
